description: Quality rules for trackers, timers, calculators, and other productivity tools.

guidelines.rule: Persist user data using localStorage. Data entered by the user MUST survive page reloads.
guidelines.why: Users lose all their data when the page refreshes if you only store in JS variables.

guidelines.rule: Include add, edit, and delete functionality for any list-based app. All three CRUD operations must work.
guidelines.why: Users frequently report that they can add items but can't edit or delete them.
examples: Add via form or inline input

guidelines.rule: Show empty states with helpful guidance. Don't show a blank screen when there's no data.
guidelines.why: A blank app with no instructions is confusing. Users don't know how to start.
examples: 'No habits tracked yet. Tap + to add your first habit.'

guidelines.rule: Expense/budget trackers MUST show a running total and a clear breakdown by category. Color-code categories. Show a balance (income minus expenses) prominently. Support adding both income and expense entries.
guidelines.why: An expense tracker that doesn't show totals or category breakdowns is just a list — it provides no value over a notes app.
examples: Bar or donut chart for category breakdown

guidelines.rule: Flashcard / study apps MUST implement spaced repetition or at minimum show cards in random order with self-rated difficulty (Easy/Hard). Show progress: X of N cards reviewed. Include a flip animation (rotateY 0→180deg) to reveal the answer.
guidelines.why: Flashcard apps that just scroll through cards in order don't aid memorization. The flip is the defining UX of the genre.
examples: Card flip: .card { transform-style: preserve-3d } .card.flipped { transform: rotateY(180deg) }
Front face: question. Back face: answer (rotateY 180deg initially)
After each flip: show 'Easy' and 'Hard' buttons — Hard cards requeue, Easy moves to next

guidelines.rule: Habit streak trackers MUST show a visual streak calendar (month view with marked days), a current streak count, and a longest streak record. Marking today as complete must update all three immediately. Support multiple habits with different colors.
guidelines.why: A streak tracker with no visual history doesn't motivate. The calendar grid + streak count is the core reward loop.
examples: Calendar grid: 7 columns (days of week), rows per week, filled circles for completed days
Streak: calculate consecutive days ending today
Longest: track max consecutive run in history

guidelines.rule: Password strength checkers MUST evaluate: length (>=8, >=12, >=16), uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols. Show a colored strength bar (red→orange→yellow→green) and specific improvement tips. Never store or transmit the password.
guidelines.why: Checkers that only use a regex for 'has uppercase+number' miss the most important factor (length). Users need actionable feedback.
examples: Score: +1 len>=8, +1 len>=12, +1 len>=16, +1 uppercase, +1 lowercase, +1 digit, +1 symbol = 0-7
Tip: 'Add symbols like !@#$%' shown only if symbols missing
